rationale: The 3-bead serial decomposition is justified and one-round verifiable: Bead 1 creates the veto, Bead 2 clears/audits it, and Bead 3 documents shipped names. The Decision slice/comparability fix is explicitly planned (plan.md:332-346; internal/panel/panel_test.go:254-256), as is the VoteDecision twin (plan.md:157-163; internal/panel/tally.go:180-214), and AC1-AC12 have named provenance rows. However, AC5's claimed exhaustive fixture reconciliation is false. internal/panel/panel_decision_test.go:211-219 is another 2A+1RC Allow fixture and will flip at leg 9.5, but is absent from both the named flips and preserving updates. cmd/mindspec/panel_test.go:557-558 and internal/instruct/panelstate_test.go:903-905 are additional at-threshold RC-filler fixtures whose derived gate outcome flips silently. The substring-superset technique does preserve the cited sub-threshold assertions (panel_decision_test.go:196-233; votedecision_test.go:72-79), but cannot preserve these Allow outcomes. Therefore the AC5 diff-review proof is not currently executable as stated.
concrete_changes_required: Extend Bead 1's exhaustive AC5 reconciliation to cover internal/panel/panel_decision_test.go:211-219, cmd/mindspec/panel_test.go:557-558, and internal/instruct/panelstate_test.go:903-905. Classify each explicitly; preferably preserve each at-threshold/Allow subject with genuine APPROVEs plus an explicit threshold equal to N, or name it as an intended semantic flip and update AC5/provenance accordingly.

Update the Bead 1 diff-review checklist and completion-note file list so these added fixtures are included; retain the existing message-substring assertions for the genuinely sub-threshold Block rows.

findings.summary: The outcome-preserving fixture inventory omits at least three RC-filler decision fixtures.
evidence: internal/panel/panel_decision_test.go:211-219 explicitly expects Allow for 2 APPROVE + 1 REQUEST_CHANGES.
cmd/mindspec/panel_test.go:557-558 labels 5A+1RC as at-threshold and derives its outcome from PanelGateDecision, so it silently changes from Allow to Block.
internal/instruct/panelstate_test.go:903-905 has the same unlisted 5A+1 neutral at-threshold fixture.
plan.md:196-223 claims an exhaustive five-item preserving list but names none of these consumers.
